title: calamagrostis arundinacea subsp. monticola
intro: Calamagrostis arundinacea subsp. monticola, also known as mountain reed grass, is a subspecies of a perennial grass native to high-altitude regions. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Calamagrostis arundinacea subsp. monticola include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory problems, as well as providing antioxidant properties that can help protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make infusions, decoctions, and teas, which are often used to relieve coughs, colds, and other respiratory ailments.
